channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump floats MAGA rally instead of concert after musicians drop out of Freedom 250
item.description: Several artists, including country singer Martina McBride, have withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.
item.content:encoded:
Several artists, including country singer Martina McBride, have withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: United Airlines flight to Spain pulls U-turn, apparently over Bluetooth device name
item.description: The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.
item.content:encoded:
The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Inside ATL: how Delta juggles 100,000 bags a day at the world's busiest airport
item.description: As the peak summer travel season gets underway, NPR gets a rare look behind the scenes at how Delta Air Lines moves more than 100,000 bags a day at the world's busiest airport in Atlanta.
item.content:encoded: As the peak summer travel season gets underway, NPR gets a rare look behind the scenes at how Delta Air Lines moves more than 100,000 bags a day at the world's busiest airport in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: DC will host America 250 celebrations — and a large deployment of the National Guard
item.description: The showy National Guard deployments have ended, but thousands of troops remain on the streets of several American cities. And these deployments come with a steep price tag.
item.content:encoded: The showy National Guard deployments have ended, but thousands of troops remain on the streets of several American cities. And these deployments come with a steep price tag.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Democrats and Republicans think control of Congress runs through Iowa
item.description: Iowa is no stranger to political attention in presidential elections, but surprisingly competitive midterm contests highlight the state's importance for both parties as it holds its primary Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
Iowa is no stranger to political attention in presidential elections, but surprisingly competitive midterm contests highlight the state's importance for both parties as it holds its primary Tuesday.

(Image credit: Stephen Fowler | NPR)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: T.V. reality stars throw their hats into the ring for political office
item.description: Reality shows are an escape with characters who can be larger than life. But some stars use the experience of fame as a stepping stone toward another challenge: running for political office.
item.content:encoded:
Reality shows are an escape with characters who can be larger than life. But some stars use the experience of fame as a stepping stone toward another challenge: running for political office.

(Image credit: Luke Gulbranson for Congress)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Immigrant detainees sue over 'horrific' conditions at Texas ICE facility
item.description: Immigrant detainees accuse the federal government of 'inhumane' abuse and treatment at Camp East Montana in El Paso, Texas.
item.content:encoded:
Immigrant detainees accuse the federal government of 'inhumane' abuse and treatment at Camp East Montana in El Paso, Texas.

(Image credit: Morgan Lee)
item.dc:creator: Kristin Wright

item.title: Judge agrees to review Trump's $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund
item.description: The fund has sparked a broad backlash since it was announced earlier this month, exposing fractures within the Republican Party.
item.content:encoded:
The fund has sparked a broad backlash since it was announced earlier this month, exposing fractures within the Republican Party.

(Image credit: China Pool)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Boston's Brazilian residents prepare to cheer on their home country in World Cup
item.description: Massachusetts has one of the largest Brazilian populations in the U.S. And all across the state, Brazilians are gearing up to watch their homeland's national team compete in the World Cup.
item.content:encoded: Massachusetts has one of the largest Brazilian populations in the U.S. And all across the state, Brazilians are gearing up to watch their homeland's national team compete in the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Esteban Bustillos

item.title: Strategies Americans are using for eating and shopping as food prices skyrocket
item.description: In the NPR series "What's Eating America," reporter Joe Hernandez examined how Americans across are adapting to high food prices and the strategies they use to cover them.
item.content:encoded: In the NPR series "What's Eating America," reporter Joe Hernandez examined how Americans across are adapting to high food prices and the strategies they use to cover them.
item.dc:creator: Gurjit Kaur
